courseleaf.title: Public Health Sciences, Doctor of Philosophy
courseleaf.informationtext:
Graduate Program

The UC Davis Ph.D. Program in Public Health Sciences prepares students to become highly trained public health research professionals and leaders in academic institutions, governmental public health agencies, and community-based organizations. The program includes two years of rigorous research coursework and rotations, followed by completion of a dissertation that enables students to apply translational public health sciences research techniques in public health practice and research settings.

This program is not STEM-designated.

Degree Requirements & Program Coordinator

See Public Health Sciences (PhD).
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The Ph.D. in public health sciences is designed to create graduates who will be experts in generating and disseminating new knowledge about health, disease prevention, and effective programs in public health. The doctoral students in this program will be educated in research design, data analysis, knowledge implementation and public health practice.
